title: Influence of a graphene substrate on the stabilization of molecular systems with hydrogen bonds
abstract: Numerical simulation of the dynamics of planar two- and three-layer molecular structures formed by $β$-sheets of polyglycine peptide chains and systems of parallel Kevlar (para-aramid) molecules placed on a graphene sheet has been performed. It is shown that in these structures the $β$-sheets retain their shape, due to the presence of parallel chains of hydrogen bonds, up to a temperature of $T=800$K. An even higher stability is exhibited by the system of parallel Kevlar molecules. Here, the parallel chains of hydrogen bonds between peptide groups of neighboring molecules are preserved even at higher temperatures. The performed modeling allows us to conclude that the addition of graphene to Kevlar fibers can significantly increase their thermal stability.
summary: This paper uses numerical simulations to study how a graphene substrate can stabilize molecular structures like polyglycine beta-sheets and Kevlar molecules. It finds that these structures can maintain their shape and hydrogen bond networks at temperatures as high as 800K. Specifically, the study suggests that adding graphene to Kevlar fibers could significantly improve their thermal resistance. This work highlights the potential for using 2D materials to enhance the properties of organic polymers.
main_problem: Investigating how a graphene substrate influences the thermal stability and structural integrity of molecular systems held together by hydrogen bonds.
main_result: The graphene substrate helps stabilize beta-sheets of polyglycine and parallel Kevlar molecules up to 800K, with Kevlar showing even higher thermal stability due to preserved hydrogen bond chains.
method: Numerical simulation of the molecular dynamics of planar multi-layer structures.
model_or_system: Planar two- and three-layer molecular structures consisting of polyglycine peptide beta-sheets and parallel Kevlar (para-aramid) molecules placed on a graphene sheet.
key_observables: Structural stability and retention of shape/hydrogen bond chains as a function of temperature.
main_assumptions: The simulation assumes the dynamics are governed by the interactions between the molecular layers and the graphene substrate.
paper_structure: The paper presents a numerical simulation study, followed by results regarding the thermal stability of peptide and Kevlar structures, and concludes with the implications for enhancing Kevlar fiber stability.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
